checks.finding: T-001/T-002/T-003/T-004/T-007 all declare Risk: high with rationale citing sentinel surfaces (T-003: secrets/signature handling REQ-008 = 'secrets handling'; T-004: server.ts tool registration exposing a new externally-callable MCP surface = REQ-001; T-007: contracts/sdd-forge-mcp-tools.v1.schema.json okEnvelope.data.oneOf addition = 'public API contracts' REQ-012; T-001/T-002: integrity-verification judgment logic feeding the Done-gate decision, matching requirements.md's own Risks section 'Risk: high' framing). None of the high tasks has a scope limited to UI/CSS/docs/test-only (all touch tools/evidence.ts or server.ts or the schema). T-005 (Risk: medium, test-only golden fixtures in tests/golden/) and T-008 (Risk: medium, determinism/smoke/dist rebuild) are correctly not over-classified to high despite touching security-relevant verification, since they add no new sensitive-surface code. T-006 (Risk: low, README/USERGUIDE + traceability addendum only) has no control-flow/data/security impact, consistent with the low tier. No under- or over-classification found.

Each Risk: high task (T-001, T-002, T-003, T-004, T-007) has a Done When item 'Red→Green evidence 記録(tdd)' and a Done When item '独立レビュー verdict PASS が evidence に記録される', matching the required-workflow: tdd derivation from risk-classification-policy.md and the risk-gate-matrix.md 'independent-review verdict' control. No task in this decomposition is Risk: critical, so the second-approver/signed-bundle requirement does not apply. No missing mandatory evidence item found on any high-risk task.

All 8 tasks have Scope sections of 1-3 implementation areas (max is T-001/T-003/T-004/T-008 at 3) and Done When lists of 4-7 checkable items (max is T-001/T-002 at 7), both within the oversizing thresholds (>3 scope areas, >8 Done-When items). No task title implies multiple sequential phases. No fragmentation pattern found: no three-or-more tasks each scoped to a single function/file edit, and no task whose entire scope is a bare rename. Each task (including single-purpose T-005 golden fixtures and T-006 docs) carries a broader integration/verification concern tied to specific REQ/AC sets, not an isolated edit.

Every functional task maps (via traceability.md Task->REQ and AC->TEST->Task) to acceptance criteria that include explicit error/edge scenarios, not only happy path: T-001 -> AC-002/003 (tamper/mismatch), AC-004/005 (missing/too-large/path-denied), AC-017 (invalid-recorded-sha), AC-018 (empty artifacts); T-002 -> AC-006 (spec drift), AC-007 (non-40-hex shape failure), AC-008 (external commit edge case), AC-009/010 (cross-binding mismatch), AC-019 (all specs absent); T-003 -> AC-011 (canary no-key leak test), AC-014 (static no-exec grep); T-007 -> AC-015 includes invalid-input/not-found/cannot-parse error envelopes. T-004's Done When explicitly requires 'エラーエンベロープ...が既存規約どおり写像される' (error-path tests green) in addition to the AC-001 happy-path pass. No functional task is happy-path-only.

Declared test types in acceptance-tests.md align with their tested scope: unit tests (TEST-007 git_commit shape regex, TEST-013 determinism of a single function call) target a single module/function boundary; integration tests (TEST-001/002/003/004/005/006/008/009/010/017/018/019) each exercise multiple components (parser + path-guard + disk I/O + hashing); golden (TEST-012) and static (TEST-008 partial, TEST-014) and smoke (TEST-016) are explicitly-typed non-functional verification categories consistent with their described scope (host-script parity, grep-based static analysis, MCP Inspector tools/list). No test task's declared type mismatches its described scope.

Every Risk: high task (T-001, T-002, T-003, T-004, T-007) carries an explicit 'Rollback:' field describing commit revert with cross-reference to design.md's Deployment/CI Plan (additive contract change, single-PR revert per ADR-0003). No Risk: high/critical task lacks a rollback consideration.

mcp/sdd-forge-mcp/src/tools/evidence.ts is incrementally extended by T-001, T-002, T-003, and T-004, but each is strictly ordered via Blockers (T-002 blocked by T-001; T-003 blocked by T-001,T-002; T-004 blocked by T-001,T-002,T-003), which is the accepted 'Blockers field enforces the ordering' pattern for legitimate multi-task scope sharing on a single evolving file. contracts/sdd-forge-mcp-tools.v1.schema.json is touched only by T-007; dist/ only by T-008; README.md/USERGUIDE.md only by T-006. No uncoordinated parallel overlap (same file, no blocking relationship) found.

Blockers trace to genuine artifact dependencies: T-002<-T-001 (extends the verdict/failures skeleton T-001 establishes), T-003<-T-001,T-002 and T-004<-T-001,T-002,T-003 (sequential same-file layering of tools/evidence.ts), T-007<-T-004 (contract schema addition needs the finalized evidenceDeepVerifyData response shape T-004 produces), T-008<-T-007 and T-005<-T-008 (final determinism/smoke/dist-rebuild and golden-parity verification exercise the fully integrated, contract-conformant tool), T-006<-all (docs/traceability finalization after every other task). The orchestrator's context notes confirm the non-numeric T-ID ordering in tasks.md is intentional. No blocker was found connecting tasks with independent scopes, and no missing blocker was found for a task whose scope depends on another's output.

SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope. All 8 tasks (T-001, T-002, T-003, T-004, T-007, T-008, T-005, T-006) implement new feature functionality for evidence_deep_verify; none is scoped as a bugfix, regression fix, debugging, failure-diagnosis, flaky-test resolution, or incident remediation task per their Goal/Scope/Done When text.
